name: okta_password_accessed
description: A user revealed another user's stored application password (application.user_membership.show_password).

validation.notes: Zero application.user_membership.show_password events in 90d. DEGRADED: the Panther rule only fires when the actor is NOT among the target Users (i.e. accessed someone else's password), comparing actor.alternateId against target[].alternateId. Honeycomb does not ingest target[].alternateId, so the actor-vs-target comparison cannot be reproduced; this spec fires on the event itself and surfaces actor + target display names for the analyst to judge. Tighten upstream if the self-access case proves noisy.
